name: Chocolate Ecstacy Cake
recipeInstructions:
Chocolate Cake:
1. Beat eggs with sugar at high speed for 5 minutes.
2. Sift together cake flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, and cocoa; fold into egg batter.
3. Pour batter into a greased and floured 10-inch pan.
4. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 1 hour or until the cake begins to separate from the sides of the pan.
5. Remove the cake while still warm and wrap in plastic wrapping. Place in the freezer.
Chocolate Butter Cream:
1. Place sugar and egg whites in the top of a double boiler and heat until sugar dissolves.
2. Beat at high speed until meringue forms peaks.
3. Gradually add softened butter and continue whipping.
4. Add vanilla and melted chocolate, then beat until well blended.
Whipped Cream Frosting:
1. Combine cream, sugar, and vanilla in a bowl; whip at high speed until stiff peaks form.
2. Assemble the cake by slicing the frozen cake horizontally into thirds.
3. Spread one-third of the buttercream on the top of the first layer.
4. Place the second layer above that and spread one-third of the buttercream on top, reserving the remaining buttercream for decoration.
5. Place the third layer in place.
6. Cover the entire cake, including sides, with whipped cream frosting.
7. Decorate the top center of the cake with shaved bits of semisweet chocolate.
8. Using a pastry bag with a medium star tip, make rosettes around the circumference of the cake using the remaining buttercream.
9. Place a small square of melted semisweet chocolate on the side of the cake.
This rich and chocolately cake can be prepared in a conventional round-shaped pan or a pretty heart-shaped one for Valentine's Day. If you don't have a heart-shaped pan, simply substitute one square and one round pan and prepare two cakes. Slice the round layer in half and arrange the halves as shown in the picture to create a heart-shaped cake.
